pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states 'Change the sampling rate to 100 Hz'
PMC/Nature paper (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9178032/) confirms ECG and PLETH waveform data in VitalDB are sampled at 500 Hz (1/500 second intervals)
Task description explicitly states '5 minutes' which equals 300 seconds; also specified in export dialog configuration 'End 300 sec'
Mathematical calculation: 100 Hz × 300 seconds = 30,000 samples. Independently verified through code execution.
Specified in task.json metadata; used by verifier.py to allow ±500 sample variations around the expected 30,000 samples. Reasonable buffer for timing variations in digital signal processing.
Task description specifies 'Select ONLY "SNUADC/ECG_II" and "SNUADC/PLETH"'. Metadata abbreviates by removing SNUADC/ prefix.

VitalDB Web API documentation (https://vitaldb.net/docs/?documentId=1jLTcF4JYbRTuSM2mZeTMmvzxMmrqUjEEp6p02cFEs_Q) specifies the download endpoint as https://vitaldb.net/api/download. The URL in metadata (api.vitaldb.net/cases/6) does not match official documentation; api.vitaldb.net responds with AWS S3 access control headers.
Consistent with setup_task.sh script which defines this as the local cache path for the downloaded VitalDB case file. Windows path format appropriate for Docker container environment.

privileged_info_summary: VitalDB Case 6 is real surgical patient data from an open, public dataset (https://vitaldb.net/) containing multi-parameter vital signs from surgical patients. The task involves downsampling ECG_II and PLETH waveforms from their original 500 Hz sampling rate to 100 Hz over a 5-minute (300-second) duration, resulting in exactly 30,000 samples per track. The sampling rate specifications have been independently verified against the peer-reviewed Nature Scientific Data paper documenting VitalDB. The official VitalDB API endpoint for downloading data is https://vitaldb.net/api/download, not the https://api.vitaldb.net/cases/6 URL claimed in metadata.
